extract.metaTitle: Extract Fujifilm Film Simulation Recipe from Photo
extract.metaDescription: Upload a Fujifilm JPEG or RAF file to automatically extract the film simulation recipe settings. Supports all Fujifilm X and GFX cameras.
extract.title: Extract Film Simulation Recipe from Your Photo
extract.subtitle: Upload any Fujifilm JPEG or RAF file and we'll automatically read the film simulation settings embedded in your photo's metadata.
extract.step1Description: Drop a JPEG or RAF file taken with any Fujifilm camera. The file contains embedded metadata with your exact film simulation settings.
extract.step2Description: We read the Fujifilm MakerNote data from your photo's EXIF to identify the camera model, lens, and all film simulation parameters.
extract.step3Description: Review the extracted settings — Film Simulation, Dynamic Range, Grain, White Balance, Tone Curve, and more — then share it with the community.
extract.ctaHint: Free to use — click the Scan button on any page to get started
extract.faq1A: Both Fujifilm JPEG (.jpg) and RAW (.raf) files are supported. The film simulation recipe is read from the MakerNote metadata embedded in the file.
extract.faq2A: Film Simulation, Dynamic Range, Grain Effect (roughness and size), Color Chrome Effect, Color Chrome FX Blue, White Balance (type, temperature, R/B shift), Highlight/Shadow tone, Color, Sharpness, Noise Reduction, Clarity, and BW adjustments.
extract.faq3Q: Does it work with photos edited in Lightroom or Capture One?
extract.faq3A: The extraction reads the original camera settings, not edits made in post-processing software. As long as the EXIF MakerNote data is preserved, extraction will work.
extract.faq4Q: Is my photo stored on the server?
extract.faq4A: Your original photo is only used for metadata extraction and is not stored. When you share a recipe, a compressed thumbnail is uploaded for display purposes.

search.metaTitle: Find Fujifilm Film Simulation Recipes by Image or Text
search.metaDescription: Upload a photo or describe the mood you want to find matching Fujifilm film simulation recipes. AI-powered search using CLIP image embeddings.
search.title: Find the Perfect Film Simulation Recipe
search.subtitle: Upload any photo to find recipes with a similar look, or describe the mood you want in words. Our AI matches your input against the community's recipe collection.
search.imageDescription: Upload any photo — from any camera or even your phone — and our AI analyzes its visual characteristics to find film simulation recipes that produce a similar look.
search.imageBullet1: Works with any photo, not just Fujifilm
search.imageBullet2: Matches color tone, contrast, and mood
search.imageBullet3: Filter by camera model for compatible settings
search.textDescription: Describe the aesthetic you're looking for in natural language. Our AI translates your description into a visual concept and finds matching recipes.
search.textBullet2: Describe mood, scene, or color palette
search.ctaHint: Sign in to search — free and unlimited
search.faq1A: We use CLIP, an AI model that understands visual concepts, to generate an embedding (a numerical representation) of your uploaded photo. This embedding is compared against embeddings of all recipe sample photos in our database using vector similarity search.
search.faq2Q: Do I need a Fujifilm photo to search?
search.faq2A: No! You can upload any photo from any camera or device. The search finds recipes whose sample photos have a similar visual style to yours.
search.faq3A: Your text description is translated to English (if needed) and converted into the same embedding space as images using CLIP. This allows us to match your text description against recipe photos by visual similarity.
search.faq4Q: Can I filter results by my camera model?
search.faq4A: Yes! Use the camera filter to narrow results to recipes compatible with your specific Fujifilm camera model and its sensor generation.
